response: 389 is a prime number. It is not divisible by any primes ≤ 19 (its square root is ~19.72), so no further checks are needed.
reasoning_content: We need to determine if 389 is a prime number. Quick sanity check: check divisibility by small primes. 389 is odd, not divisible by 2. Sum of digits 3+8+9=20, not divisible by 3, so not divisible by 3. Last digit not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*55=385, remainder 4, so not divisible. Check 11: 11*35=385, remainder 4, not divisible. Check 13: 13*29=377, remainder 12; 13*30=390, too high, so not divisible. Check 17: 17*22=374, remainder 15; 17*23=391, too high. Check 19: 19*20=380, remainder 9; 19*21=399, too high. Check 23: 23*16=368, remainder 21; 23*17=391, too high. Check 29: 29*13=377, remainder 12; 29*14=406 too high. Check 31: 31*12=372, remainder 17; 31*13=403 too high. Check 37: 37*10=370, remainder 19; 37*11=407 too high. Check 41: 41*9=369, remainder 20; 41*10=410 too high. Check 43: 43*9=387, remainder 2; 43*10=430. Check 47: 47*8=376, remainder 13; 47*9=423. Check 53: 53*7=371, remainder 18; 53*8=424. Check 59: 59*6=354, remainder 35; 59*7=413. Check 61: 61*6=366, remainder 23; 61*7=427. Check 67: 67*5=335, remainder 54; 67*6=402. Check 71: 71*5=355, remainder 34; 71*6=426. Check 73: 73*5=365, remainder 24; 73*6=438. Check 79: 79*4=316, remainder 73; 79*5=395. Check 83: 83*4=332, remainder 57; 83*5=415. Check 89: 89*4=356, remainder 33; 89*5=445. Check 97: 97*4=388, remainder 1; so not divisible. The square root of 389 is about 19.72, so we only need to check primes up to 19. Actually, sqrt(389) ≈ 19.72, so we only need to check primes up to 19. We already checked 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19. None divide evenly. So 389 is prime. Quick sanity: 389 mod 19 = 9, not zero. So yes, prime.
